item.name: 🚀 Testing Guide & Docs
item.description:
## Welcome to sya API Testing Guide

Follow these detailed steps to test APIs and Socket events:

### 1. Setup & Auth (First Step)
1.  **Login**: First, go to **Auth Module > 1.1 Login**.
2.  **Auto-Token**: Successful login korle `accessToken` automatic collection variable-e save hoye jabe.
3.  **Check**: Apni Collection-er **Variables** tab-e giye check korte paren token-ti set hoyeche kina.

### 2. Standard API Testing
- Protiti folder module onujayi organized.
- Path variables (যেমন `:id`) thakle Postman-er **Params** tab-e value boshate hobe.

### 3. Socket.IO Testing (Detailed Guide)
Postman theke Socket test korar jonno nicher visual guide-ti follow koren:

#### A. Connection Setup
- Click **New** > **Socket.IO**.
- **URL**: `http://localhost:5002` (Not `/api/v1`).
- **Auth (Handshake)**:
    - **Handshake** tab-e jan.
    - **Auth** section-e **Auth Object** select koren.
    - `token` key-te value `{{accessToken}}` boshie **Connect** koren.

#### B. How to "Listen" (Receive events from server)
Server theke real-time data receive korar jonno nicher steps follow koren:

1.  **Events Tab**: Connection tab-er pashe thaka **Events** tab-e jan.
2.  **Add Event**: `Add event` button-e click koren.
3.  **Event Name**: `Event name` field-e nicher common event-gulo add koren (ekekti event-er jonno alada row):
    - `USER_ONLINE`: Jokhon keu online ashe.
    - `MESSAGE_RECEIVED`: Jokhon keu apnake message pathay.
    - `TYPING_START`: Keu type kora shuru korle.
    - `get-notification::{{userId}}`: Personal notifications-er jonno.
4.  **Messages Pane**: Ekhon server theke oi event-ti asle niche **Messages** pane-e real-time JSON data dekhabe. 
    > **Note**: Ekhane kono JSON pathano lagbe na, shudhu event-er nam-ti add korlei Postman server theke asha data capture korbe.

#### C. How to "Emit" (Send events to server)
- Server-e data pathanor jonno **Message** tab-e jan.
- **Event Name**: Jekhare event-er nam likhte hoy (যেমন: `JOIN_CHAT`).
- **Payload**: Niche JSON format-e data-ti likhun:
    ```json
    { "chatId": "664a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e" }
    ```
- Click **Send**.

### 🔌 Socket.IO Events Inventory & Guide

Jokhon apni Socket test korben, nicher table-ti follow koren bujhar jonno je konta apnake pathate hobe (Emit) ar konta server pathabe (Listen).

| Event Name | Action Type | Direction | Payload Example | Step-by-Step Testing |
|------------|-------------|-----------|-----------------|----------------------|
| `JOIN_CHAT` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | `{ "chatId": "66..." }` | **Message** tab-e event name `JOIN_CHAT` likhun ebong payload JSON format-e diye **Send** koren. |
| `LEAVE_CHAT` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `LEAVE_CHAT` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `JOIN_TICKET` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `JOIN_TICKET` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `LEAVE_TICKET` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `LEAVE_TICKET` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `TYPING_START` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | `{ "chatId": "66..." }` | **Message** tab theke emit koren typing shuru bujhate. |
| `TYPING_STOP` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `TYPING_STOP` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `DELIVERED_ACK` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `DELIVERED_ACK` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `READ_ACK` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `READ_ACK` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `disconnect` | **EMITTING** (Send) | 📥 Client -> Server | N/A | **Message** tab-e event name `disconnect` likhe JSON payload shoho **Send** koren. |
| `TYPING_START` | **LISTENING** (Receive) | 📤 Server -> Client | `{ "chatId": "66..." }` | **Events** tab-e `TYPING_START` add koren listening-er jonno. |
| `TYPING_STOP` | **LISTENING** (Receive) | 📤 Server -> Client | N/A | **Events** tab-e `TYPING_STOP` nam-er ekta event add koren jate server theke response ashole apni **Messages** pane-e dekhte paren. |


### 4. Common Testing Scenarios
- **Chatting**:
    1. Login koren.
    2. Socket connect koren.
    3. `JOIN_CHAT` emit koren chatId diye.
    4. Onno user theke message pathale apni listener events-e sheta paben.

request.description: Standard email + password sign-in. Returns an access-token / refresh-token pair. The refresh token is also set as an `httpOnly` cookie so browser clients don't have to handle it in JS. Status checks happen at the service layer **before** password comparison so the user gets the right reason (suspended / rejected / unverified) instead of a generic 401.

body.raw:
{
  "email": "user@example.com", // Required: Yes
  "password": "P@ssw0rd!", // Required: Yes
  "deviceToken": "fcm-or-apns-token-optional", // Required: No
  "platform": "ios", // Required: No
  "appVersion": "1.0.0", // Required: No
}

exec: console.log("accessToken has been set in collection variables");

request.description: Single endpoint that handles **two distinct flows** based on the user's `isVerified` state at the moment of OTP submission:

exec: console.log("accessToken has been set in collection variables");

request.description: Initiates the password-reset flow by sending a 6-digit OTP to the user's email. Designed with **silent success** — the response is identical whether the email belongs to a real account or not. An attacker who pastes a list of emails into this endpoint cannot tell which ones are registered. The actual reset happens in two more steps: [02-verify-otp.md](./02-verify-otp.md) (issues a `resetToken`) → [04-reset-password.md](./04-reset-password.md) (commits the new password).

Commits a new password using the `resetToken` issued by [02-verify-otp.md](./02-verify-otp.md). The token sits in the `Authorization` header (either as raw value or with `Bearer ` prefix — controller accepts both). On success: password is hashed, the user's `tokenVersion` is bumped (every JWT they hold becomes invalid — true global logout), the one-time `isResetPassword` flag is cleared, and every reset token tied to this user is deleted.

Rotates a refresh token. Verifies the inbound refresh JWT, compares its embedded `tokenVersion` against the DB value (**reuse detection**), bumps the DB `tokenVersion`, and issues a fresh access + refresh pair. If a previously-rotated refresh token is replayed (typical attacker behavior with a stolen token after the legitimate client has already rotated), the version check fails and every JWT for the user is invalidated — the user must log in again.

exec: console.log("accessToken has been set in collection variables");

request.description: Ends the **current device's** push session and clears the `refreshToken` browser cookie. Removes the supplied `deviceToken` from the user's `deviceTokens[]` array so the device stops receiving FCM/APNs notifications. **Does NOT bump `tokenVersion`** — the JWT itself remains valid until natural expiry (short-lived access token; refresh-token flow will continue to work on this device until the user logs back in elsewhere). To globally invalidate every session on every device, use [user/12-revoke-all-sessions.md](../user/12-revoke-all-sessions.md) (bumps `tokenVersion`).

Re-issues the 6-digit registration verification OTP that gets sent during account creation. **Only for the registration flow** — to obtain a fresh password-reset OTP, call [03-forgot-password.md](./03-forgot-password.md) again, which overwrites the existing OTP and invalidates prior reset tokens.

Unified sign-in / sign-up via Google or Apple ID tokens. Verifies the provider-issued JWT cryptographically (Google: RSA against `googleAudience` array of iOS/Android/Web client IDs; Apple: RSA + nonce-hash match), then either logs an existing user in (matched strictly by `googleId` / `appleId`) or creates a new account. Refuses to auto-link a provider identity to an existing email-based account — that's an OWASP-flagged account-hijack vector. Returns the same `{ accessToken, refreshToken }` envelope as [01-login.md](./01-login.md).

body.raw:
{
  "provider": "google", // Required: Yes
  "idToken": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIs...", // Required: Yes
  "nonce": "aB3xK9mP2qR7sL4nT6vY8wXcZ1uV5oM0jE", // Required: Conditional
  "deviceToken": "fcm-token-optional", // Required: No
  "platform": "ios", // Required: No
  "appVersion": "1.4.0", // Required: No
}

request.description: For an already-logged-in user who wants to change their password without going through the email OTP flow. Validates the current password (defense-in-depth against stolen-token mutation), enforces the password-strength regex on the new password, hashes and stores it, and **bumps `tokenVersion`** — every JWT the user holds becomes invalid on the next request (true global logout). The client must re-authenticate after success.

Restores an account that was soft-deleted via [user/06-delete-account.md](../user/06-delete-account.md) and is still inside its 30-day recovery window. The endpoint takes `email` + `password`, validates both, flips `status: DELETED -> ACTIVE`, clears `deletedAt` / `recoveryDeadline`, bumps `tokenVersion` (so any leftover JWTs from before deletion stay invalid), and issues a fresh access + refresh token pair — same response shape as `POST /auth/login`.

Single endpoint for both **public mobile registration** (`BROTHER` / `SISTER`) and **administrative account creation** (`SUPER_ADMIN`). The difference is detected at the controller level: if a valid `Bearer` token belonging to a `SUPER_ADMIN` is supplied, the request is treated as admin creation; otherwise it is public registration. There is **no `auth` middleware** on this route — the JWT is inspected inline. Any token error silently falls back to public registration.

formdata.description: The user's full legal name as it should appear on their profile.
Primary email address. Must be globally unique and valid.
Account password. Must be 8+ chars with mixed case, digit, and special char.
User role. Limited to `BROTHER` or `SISTER` during public registration.
The date the user converted to Islam in Full ISO 8601 format.
User's birth date in Full ISO 8601 format. Must be 16+ years old.
Government ID or proof image for admin verification.
Short face-verification video for admin review.
A short biography or intro about the user.
The user's personal story of converting to Islam.
Array of strings representing user interests.

request.description: Retrieves public profile information of another user.
Returns the **full** profile of the currently authenticated user, including private fields hidden from the public endpoint. Read-only — no DB writes, no side effects.
Allows the currently authenticated user to update their own profile (text fields and an optional new `profileImage`). The previous profile image is unlinked from disk when a new one is uploaded.

formdata.description: A short biography or intro about the user.
The user's personal story of converting to Islam.
The date the user converted to Islam in Full ISO 8601 format.
Array of strings representing user interests.

request.description: Lets the authenticated user request deletion of their own account. The account is **soft-deleted** with a 30-day recovery window — during that window the user can come back and re-activate via [auth/10-restore-account.md](../auth/10-restore-account.md). After 30 days, a daily cron job at 03:00 UTC permanently removes the user record and cascade-deletes their owned content (notifications, group activity, ask-imam questions, reset tokens). Subscription history is intentionally retained for billing / IAP-refund audit.

Step 1 of the self-service email-change flow. The user supplies a new email address and their current password; the server validates the password, checks the new email isn't already in use, stores the pending change on the user document, and:

Step 2 of the self-service email-change flow. The user submits the 6-digit OTP that was emailed to the **new** address by [07-email-change-request.md](./07-email-change-request.md). On a successful match the server commits the change: `email` is updated to the pending value, the `emailChange` subdoc is cleared, and `tokenVersion` is bumped — invalidating every JWT that was issued under the old email. The client must call [auth/01-login.md](../auth/01-login.md) again with the new email.

Returns a single JSON envelope containing every piece of data the system stores **about** the requesting user. Satisfies GDPR Article 15 (right of access). Synchronous — the entire payload is in the HTTP response. Mobile-store policies (Apple §5.1.1(v), Google Play account-deletion policy) require this surface to exist for accounts in EU jurisdictions.

Lists every device session for the authenticated user. A "session" is one entry in `User.deviceTokens[]` — typically created at login on each device (mobile / tablet / browser). The response returns metadata only: **the raw FCM/APNs push token is never exposed**, only the subdoc id (`tokenId`), `platform`, `appVersion`, and `lastSeenAt`. The client uses `tokenId` to address a specific session in [11-revoke-session.md](./11-revoke-session.md).

Removes **one** specific device session from the authenticated user's `deviceTokens[]`. The device stops receiving push notifications immediately. The JWT issued to that device **remains valid until natural expiry** — `tokenVersion` is intentionally not bumped (bumping it would invalidate every session, not just this one). For a true "logout this device, invalidate its token now" use [auth/06-logout.md](../auth/06-logout.md) **on the device itself**, or use [12-revoke-all-sessions.md](./12-revoke-all-sessions.md) to nuke everything.

Nukes every device session for the authenticated user — clears `deviceTokens[]` entirely **and** bumps `tokenVersion`. Every JWT this user holds (including the one used to call this endpoint) becomes invalid on the next request. The classic "log me out everywhere" / "I lost my phone" button.

Recovery path for users whose `status` was flipped to `REJECTED` by an admin. Because REJECTED users are blocked by both the login service and the protected-route auth middleware (see [auth/01-login.md](../auth/01-login.md) §status table and [system-concepts.md](../../system-concepts.md)), they have no way to authenticate. This endpoint is **public** and authenticates the user via a one-time token that the admin reject flow emails them automatically (see [admin/07-update-user.md](../admin/07-update-user.md) — `accountRejected` template).

formdata.description: 64-char hex token from the rejection email

request.description: Lists all users, with search, various filters, and paginated results. Also used for the Approval Queue by filtering with `status=PENDING`.
Aggregated growth metrics for the admin dashboard.
Retrieves full user details by ID. This endpoint is strictly for Administrators. Regular users should use `/users/:userId/public` or `/users/me`.
Update any user field, including their account status (ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, etc.).
Permanent hard removal of a user account.
Unified endpoint for approving or rejecting a pending user account.

query.description: Use `new-reverts` for newest members or `nearby-me` for location-based sorting

request.description: Lists public profiles of users within the same community. Brothers see other Brothers, and Sisters see other Sisters. Only active users are listed. Includes distance calculation if coordinates are provided. **Each profile item includes a `connectionStatus` flag so the frontend can conditionally render the Connect button.**
**Response format**: See [Standard Response Envelope](../../README.md#standard-response-envelope)

formdata.description: The text content of the question (Max 2000)

request.description: Admins can create new user groups with gender restrictions and categories.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Quran Study Circle", // Name of the group
  "description": "A group for brothers to study Quran", // Detailed description
  "userType": "BROTHER", // `BROTHER` or `SISTER`
  "category": "Quran Studies", // Category name (e.g., Quran Studies)
  "coverImage": "https://storage.com/group-cover.jpg"
}

request.description: Fetches a paginated list of groups. Results are automatically filtered based on the logged-in user's role.
Allows a user to join a group.
Members can create posts with text and optional attachments within a group. This endpoint supports both JSON payloads and `multipart/form-data` file uploads.

formdata.description: Optional attachment URLs or files to upload (Max 5)

request.description: Fetches the paginated list of posts for a specific group.
Toggles a like on a post. If already liked, it will be unliked.
Members can add comments to a post.
Deletes a post and all its associated likes and comments. Also cleans up any attached files.
Deletes a comment. If it's a top-level comment, all nested replies will also be deleted.
Removes the authenticated user from the specified group and decrements the group's member count.
Updates the metadata of an existing group.
Deletes a group and all associated members, posts, likes, and comments. Also cleans up attached files.
Fetches metadata for a single group, including a flag indicating if the requesting user is a member.
Fetches the paginated list of comments for a specific post.
Removes a member from a group. Only accessible by Super Admins.
Toggles the "pinned" status of a post. Pinned posts appear at the top of the group feed.
Members can edit their own posts with text and optional attachments. This endpoint supports both JSON payloads and `multipart/form-data` file uploads.

formdata.description: Optional attachment URLs or files to upload (Max 5)
Optional list of existing attachment URLs to retain

request.description: Uploads a new Jummah Khutba with audio and thumbnail.

query.description: Search by title, imam, or mosqueName

request.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all Khutbahs with search and filter options.
Retrieves detailed information about a specific Khutba by its ID.
Updates an existing Khutba. Supports partial updates and optional file replacements.
Permanently deletes a Khutba and its associated files.
Adds a new mosque to the database with location, prayer times, and an optional image.

query.description: Search by name, area, address, or description

request.description: Fetches a paginated list of mosques with optional search and filtering.
Retrieves full details of a specific mosque by its ID.
Updates specific fields of an existing mosque entry. Supports partial updates and optional image replacement.
Permanently removes a mosque entry and its associated image from the database.
Marks a single notification as read. This is typically called immediately when a user taps on a notification.
Marks all unread notifications for the logged-in user as read. Typically triggered when a user taps a "Mark all as read" button.
Send a notification to a specific audience (All users, Brothers, Sisters, or Super Admins).
Retrieve a log of all broadcast notifications sent by admins.
Shob available legal pages (Terms, Privacy, etc.) er title ebong slug list fetch korar jonno. Admin-side e same endpoint use kora hoy CMS list view-er jonno.
Slug diye specific legal page-er full HTML/Markdown content fetch korar jonno.
Notun legal page create kore.
Existing legal page update kore.
Legal page delete kore.
This endpoint is used for dashboard summary cards. It calculates monthly growth: current month vs last month.
**Response format**: See [Standard Response Envelope](../../README.md#standard-response-envelope)
Admin new learning content (video) create korar jonno ei endpoint use korbe.
Shob learning content browse korar jonno. Search ebong filter support kore.
Specific content details dekhar jonno.
Admin existing content update korar jonno use korbe.

formdata.description: Updated duration of the video in seconds

request.description: Admin content delete korar jonno use korbe. Automatic likes ebong comments o delete hoye jabe.
Toggles a like on learning content. Jodi age theke like thake, tobe unlike hoye jabe.
Users can add comments or reply to existing comments.
Specific content er shob comments dekhar jonno.
User nijek comment delete korte pare, athoba Admin jekono comment delete korte pare.
**Response format**: See [Standard Response Envelope](../../README.md#standard-response-envelope)

query.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include

request.description: **Response format**: See [Standard Response Envelope](../../README.md#standard-response-envelope)
**Response format**: See [Standard Response Envelope](../../README.md#standard-response-envelope)
**Response format**: See [Standard Response Envelope](../../README.md#standard-response-envelope)
**Response format**: See [Standard Response Envelope](../../README.md#standard-response-envelope)
Note: the service does **not** verify that the provided `adminId` actually corresponds to a `SUPER_ADMIN` user — that check could be added later if assignment to non-admins becomes a concern.
`403 Forbidden` (`"message": "You don't have permission to access this API"`).
`403 Forbidden` (`"message": "You don't have permission to access this API"`).
`403 Forbidden` (`"message": "You don't have permission to access this API"`).
Allows a user to send a connection request to another user. If the request is successful, a pending connection record is created, and the receiver is notified via Socket.IO and Push Notification.
Allows the receiver of a connection request to accept it. This action updates the connection status to `ACCEPTED` and automatically creates a private chat between the two users.
Allows the receiver of a connection request to reject it. This action deletes the connection record from the database.
Allows the **sender** of a pending connection request to cancel (undo) it before the receiver responds.
Allows either user in an **accepted** connection to permanently remove it.
Fetches a paginated list of all established (ACCEPTED) connections for the logged-in user.

query.description: Whether to fetch requests you `sent` or `received`
Base64 encoded cursor value of the last item from the previous page for cursor pagination

request.description: Fetches a cursor-paginated list of pending connection requests, either sent by the user (`direction=sent`) or received by them (`direction=received`).
Creates a new chat between the logged-in user and another user, or retrieves an existing one if it already exists.
Fetches all chat conversations for the logged-in user, including last message preview, unread count, and participant presence.
Sends a text or media message within a chat conversation. Supports multi-file uploads for images, videos, and documents.
User tar nijer current subscription status, plan, expiry date ityadi dekhbar jonno ei endpoint call korbe.
iOS app theke purchase transaction verify korar jonno ei endpoint use kora hoy. Apple StoreKit theke pawa `signedTransactionInfo` pathate hobe.
Apple App Store Server Notifications V2 handle korar jonno endpoint. Subscription renewal, cancellation, grace period ityadi events Apple theke direct ei endpoint-e ashe.
Android app theke purchase verify korar jonno ei endpoint use kora hoy. Google Play Billing Client theke pawa `purchaseToken` ebong `productId` pathate hobe.
Google Play Real-Time Developer Notifications (RTDN) handle korar jonno endpoint. Subscription renewal, cancellation ityadi events Google Pub/Sub theke push message hishebe ei endpoint-e ashe.
User manually FREE plan-e switch korar jonno ei endpoint use korbe (jodi tar kono active store subscription na thake).

query.description: Latitude of the location (must be between -90 and 90)
Longitude of the location (must be between -180 and 180)
Target date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format (defaults to current date)

request.description: Computes highly accurate Salat (Prayer) times dynamically and offline based on geographical coordinates (GPS) and a date.
